g.text: ### QUERY OPTIMIZER: PREDICATE PUSHDOWN DECISION LOGIC

text.tspan: ### QUERY OPTIMIZER: PREDICATE PUSHDOWN DECISION LOGIC

g.title: Input: Filter condition expression tree (ExprTree)
Check if expression contains 0 column references (e.g., 1=1, 5 > 10)
Predicates without column references are evaluated at the Join level or handled by Constant Folding
Bitmask check: (expr.columns & right_child.schema) == 0
Update parent filter to remove this clause; inject Filter node into Left child path
Bitmask check: (expr.columns & left_child.schema) == 0
Update parent filter; inject Filter node into Right child path
Expression intersects both Left and Right relation schemas
Is this predicate already represented in the ON clause equivalence set?
Drop clause to avoid redundant evaluation overhead
Must be evaluated at the join junction; cannot be pushed down
Expression includes columns not found in child sub-trees (e.g., Correlated Outer Ref)

g.text: | Bit Offset | Field Name | Description | Size |
If `(Mask & 0x03) == 0x03`, predicate is treated as a `JOIN_FILTER`.

text.tspan: | Bit Offset | Field Name | Description | Size |
If `(Mask & 0x03) == 0x03`, predicate is treated as a `JOIN_FILTER`.
